page: Microsoft Azure DC48ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The processor runs at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and is supported by 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

The instance includes 1843 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. The AMD EPYC processor features L1d cache of 32 KB, L2 cache of 0.5 MB, and L3 cache of 32 MB. Hardware-level capabilities include Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), cryptography acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, as well as virtualization support through AMD SVM.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, scoring 64744.56 in stress-ng multi-core tests and 52188.41 in PassMark CPU tests, placing it in the top 25% tier. Cryptographic operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a strong PassMark CPU encryption score of 51369.67. Multi-threaded decompression and image processing also rank in the top 25%. However, memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier, and large-scale LLM text generation using a 70B model is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 10% with 2.33 tokens per second.

Given its dedicated compute resources, high-capacity local storage, and balanced memory allocation, the DC48ads_cc_v5 offers solid resource density for multi-threaded workloads. It is well-suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and local database hosting. The lack of a GPU and the poor performance on large-scale LLM text generation make it unsuitable for heavy deep learning inference or training tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure DC48ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and running under Microsoft Hyper-V, it is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory and 1843 GB of local storage. Benchmark results highlight strong multi-core CPU performance, placing it in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. Cryptographic operations and multi-threaded decompression are highly efficient, while memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-capacity local storage, this instance is optimized for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, software compilation, and database hosting, though it is not suited for large-scale LLM text generation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC48ads_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage, optimized for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.

tagline: A dedicated 48-vCPU AMD EPYC instance with balanced memory and high-capacity local storage for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.

192 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
1843 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
